trivia_bullets.text: Nylea lore: Nylea is the God of the Hunt, Seasons, and Wilds on Theros, inspired by Artemis/Diana of Greek mythology. Her divine weapon is Ephixis, a short bow said to never miss. Per MTG Wiki, she was briefly imprisoned in beast form by Xenagos during the original Theros block, and her symbol is a set of four arrows. Nylea's Forerunner is listed explicitly in the MTG Wiki's in-game references as an 'Associated card' for Nylea. [MTG Wiki: Nylea, retrieved via curl 2026-05-26]

Flavor text: 'Where its feet tread, the thunder of many others will follow.' This unattributed line reflects Nylea's dominion over herd beasts and the unstoppable momentum of nature in mass — the beast runs first so the rest can run behind it. The 'thunder of many others' mirrors the card's trample-granting ability: when the forerunner tramples through, so does every creature behind it. [Scryfall: flavor_text field, card thb/186]

Mechanical reputation: Nylea's Forerunner is an Enchantment Creature — Beast with 5/3 power and trample, granting trample to all other creatures you control for 5 mana. EDHREC rank 9,700 places it considerably above average for a common (contrast Glory Bearers at rank 25,396). EDHREC data shows top Commander inclusion in Slinza, the Spiked Stampede (34% of decks), Zilortha, Strength Incarnate (5.8%), and Bello, Bard of the Brambles (2.8%) — all heavy-trample-matters commanders. Legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander. [Scryfall: edhrec_rank=9700; EDHREC: edhrec.com/cards/nyleas-forerunner via WebSearch]

Artist: Christopher Burdett has specialized in designing and illustrating monsters for tabletop games since 2005, with credits across Magic, D&D, Pathfinder, and Star Wars. He illustrated Nylea's Forerunner for THB, as well as Shimmerwing Chimera from the same set. He sells signed, numbered artist proofs of this card — limited to an edition of 50 — via his own storefront, a practice that reflects his investment in the specific art as a portfolio piece. [christopherburdett.myshopify.com/products/nyleas-forerunner-artist-proof; artofmtg.com/art/nyleas-forerunner/]
sources: christopherburdett.myshopify.com: Nylea's Forerunner artist proof listing

trivia_bullets.text: Enchantment-creature frame: Like Glory Bearers and the other Theros creature commons with this treatment, Nylea's Forerunner carries the enchantment-creature dual type that signals Nyx-born or divinely marked status in Theros's cosmology — where the gods' constellations are woven directly into mortal flesh. The constellation sparkles on the creature's torso visible in the card art are the visual embodiment of this mechanic. [Scryfall: type_line='Enchantment Creature — Beast'; MTG Wiki: Theros]
